title: delivery.rs attempts column read as u32 but written back as i32
description:
The pass-1 fix changed process_batch (line 138) to read attempts as u32, but handle_retry (line 341) casts next_attempt back to i32 via `next_attempt as i32` before binding. Meanwhile, inspect() on line 376 still reads attempts as i32.

What goes wrong: a contributor sees u32 in one path and i32 in the other, and may extend MAX_ATTEMPTS beyond i32::MAX (unlikely but the inconsistency is a trap). More practically, someone adding a new query against attempts will have to guess which type to use.

Fix: pick one type (u32 is semantically correct since attempts cannot be negative) and use it consistently across all three sites: process_batch read, handle_retry write, and inspect read.

Locations:
- /home/mark/PROJECT/broadside/src/delivery.rs line 138 (reads u32)
- /home/mark/PROJECT/broadside/src/delivery.rs line 341 (writes as i32)
- /home/mark/PROJECT/broadside/src/delivery.rs line 376 (reads i32)
close_reason: Unified attempts type to i32 throughout (query, handle_retry, bind). MAX_ATTEMPTS also i32.
